page: Amazon Web Services c9g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring external storage solutions for persistent data. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The absence of a graphics processing unit and local NVMe storage represents a clear tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on high-density CPU compute capacity.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated bare-metal execution provide high resource density for multi-threaded workloads without hyperthreading overhead. The server is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, and large-scale containerized microservices that benefit from high core counts and a 100 Gbps network interface.
description: The Amazon Web Services c9g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server utilizing the arm64 architecture. Powered by AWS Graviton processors, it delivers 192 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This server does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external storage options and focusing its hardware profile entirely on raw CPU execution. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Given its high core density and dedicated resource allocation, the c9g.metal-48xl is structured for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and large-scale containerized deployments where high-throughput networking and dedicated arm64 processing cores are required.
